title: Running 128 Coding Agents at Once
summary: Sam Whitmore from Cursor discusses with Baseten's Charlie O'Neill and Harry Partridge the scaling of coding agents to high parallelism levels. The conversation covers running hundreds of agents, enabling inter-agent communication and review, and designing systems that go beyond simple task parallelization.
writeup: Sam Whitmore of Cursor talks with Baseten's Charlie O'Neill and Harry Partridge about scaling coding agents to high parallelism, including running 128 agents at once. The discussion covers orchestrating hundreds of agents, enabling inter-agent communication and review, and designing systems that move past simple task parallelization. The takeaways center on multi-agent coordination and test-time compute as levers for coding work, rather than treating agents as isolated workers splitting tasks.
